data.title: As a DSO maintainer, I can run an automated integration test suite to verify the reference library's integrity, retrieval accuracy, and performance
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Creates tests/integration/test-ref-library-integration.sh with all six sub-tests specified in SC5. Uses REPO_ROOT-based paths so it works correctly from any worktree session.

**Why**: SC5 is a first-class deliverable that validates the entire corpus and retrieval infrastructure end-to-end. This test suite is the authoritative signal that the epic's implementation is complete and correct.

**Scope**:
- IN: tests/integration/test-ref-library-integration.sh (named to match test-*-integration.sh glob for runner discovery); all 6 sub-tests; REPO_ROOT paths; 10-second timing constraint
- OUT: Unit tests for individual stories (handled within each story's implementation-plan); downstream integration epic tests

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, tests/integration/test-ref-library-integration.sh exists, is executable, and all 6 sub-tests pass:
  (a) session-timeout query returns an entry tagged domain: [auth] or containing "session timeout" in Summary tier
  (b) --tier=summary output contains ### Summary and does NOT contain ### Implementation
  (c) manifest integrity — every _index.yaml entry resolves to a file at its declared path
  (d) no corpus file exceeds 500 lines
  (e) precision sub-test: "USWDS form validation" returns top result with component: [form]; "WCAG 2.2 keyboard navigation" returns top result with compliance: [wcag-2.2-aaa] AND action: [keyboard-nav]; "government authentication anti-pattern" returns top result with domain: [auth]
  (f) entire test suite completes within 10 seconds
  ← Satisfies: SC5

- When this story is complete, tests use REPO_ROOT=$(git rev-parse --show-toplevel) for all path references; tests pass when run from a worktree session at a different path
  ← Satisfies: SC5 (REPO_ROOT-based paths requirement)

- When this story is complete, the test file is discovered and executed by tests/integration/run-integration-tests.sh (the runner uses glob test-*-integration.sh; this file matches)
  ← Satisfies: SC5 (runner discoverability)

## Considerations

- [Testing] Sub-test (e) precision expected-tag values must match tags declared in Stories 2-4 done definitions — any divergence between corpus tag choices and test assertions causes false failures
- [Reliability] Sub-test (f) timing depends on full corpus size; if total test suite exceeds 10 seconds after full corpus population, investigate BM25 loading performance before failing

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
